context.story_engine: Severed Lumon employees live split existences — their work selves trapped inside a corporate floor with no outside memories — and slowly uncover what the company is hiding while their outside selves are manipulated by forces they can't see. Breach: Employees know what their job actually is.

plotlines.name: Helly: Escape from Severance
plotlines.goal: freedom from the severed floor — to quit Lumon and cease existing as a severed innie

plotlines.name: Mark: Uncovering the Truth
plotlines.goal: understand what Lumon is hiding — what severance really does, what happened to Petey, and why his wife Gemma connects to Lumon

plotlines.name: Dylan: Disillusionment with Lumon
plotlines.goal: validation and reward from Lumon — then, once disillusioned, something real (his son, autonomy)

plotlines.name: Cobel: Maintaining Control
plotlines.goal: keep the severed floor stable, suppress rebellion, and protect Lumon's secrets from exposure

episodes.theme: Consent is meaningless when the self who agreed and the self who suffers the consequences are not the same person.

events.event: Mark leads his team of four severed employees at Lumon Industries working on refining mysterious numbers on computers
Dylan discovers a music player and dance experience in a restricted area, triggering security protocols
Helly undergoes orientation with Ms. Cobel as a new severed employee
Helly struggles to accept her severed state during orientation
Irving explains the four tempers theory to the team
Helly attempts to leave the building but discovers she cannot access the elevator without her outie's consent
Mark's outie attends a book reading and meets a woman named Alexa
Mr. Milchick leads the team in wellness sessions and monitors their behavior
Helly tries to hurt herself to send a message to her outie, prompting security intervention
Mark defends Helly to management while questioning the ethics of the severance process
The team discovers that Petey, Mark's former colleague, has left the company under mysterious circumstances
Ms. Cobel observes the team's dynamics while maintaining her corporate facade
Helly's outie sends her a message refusing to quit, leaving Helly trapped in the severed workplace
Mark begins experiencing brief flashes suggesting his memories may be bleeding through the severance barrier

interactions.description: All three plotlines turn on the same question: who owns a person's consciousness? Helly's outie refuses to free her, Mark starts questioning Lumon's ethics, and Cobel works to keep both contained — three angles on corporate sovereignty over the self.
The viewer watches Cobel monitor Helly's every move; Helly believes her self-harm and resistance are acts of agency, unaware they are being observed, logged, and managed as data points.
Mark defends Helly and senses something is wrong with Lumon, but has no idea that his supervisor Cobel is actively shaping his reality — the viewer sees both sides of this asymmetry.
Neither Mark nor Helly — nor the viewer — yet knows who Helly's outie is or why she consented to severance, making her refusal to quit a mystery to all parties.

episodes.theme: Containment is only an illusion — every wall Lumon builds is already being tested from both sides.

events.event: Helly attempts various escape methods from the Lumon offices, resisting her severed state
Mark receives a call from his outie's friend mentioning someone named Petey wanting to meet
Dylan works on refinement duties and boasts about his Lumon perks and productivity scores to colleagues
Irving shares wellness tips with colleagues during his work routine
Ms. Cobel questions Mark about any unusual experiences or memory bleeds he might be having
Helly discovers the conference room painting of Lumon founder Kier Eagan and learns about the company's reverence for him
The team participates in a Lumon orientation video covering workplace safety and corporate philosophy
Mark's outie meets with Petey, who reveals he has undergone an illegal procedure to reintegrate his severed and outside memories
Petey warns Mark about the true nature of Lumon and what happens on the severed floor
Helly attempts to use the stairwell but finds it blocked by security systems
Ms. Cobel demonstrates increasing interest in Mark's personal life and psychological state
The team completes their daily refinement quotas while Helly questions the purpose of the number-sorting work
Petey shows Mark evidence suggesting something sinister about the severance process and Lumon's actual operations
Mark's outie begins experiencing strange symptoms after his encounter with Petey

interactions.description: Cobel probes Mark for memory bleeds while the viewer knows Petey is already feeding Mark information from outside — Cobel doesn't yet know the outie meeting is happening

Both Helly and Mark are reaching for the same thing — knowledge of what lies beyond their current existence — but from opposite directions: Helly pushes out from the inside, Mark receives information pulled in from the outside
Mark's outie is meeting a reintegrated former employee and receiving dangerous intelligence about Lumon — Cobel is watching Mark closely but is blind to the outie-level breach already in progress

The orientation video scene places Helly's active resistance alongside Dylan's comfortable compliance — two employees receiving identical institutional conditioning who are at opposite ends of belief in Lumon

episodes.theme: Lumon's control depends on keeping its people sealed inside their own ignorance — and the seals are starting to leak

events.event: Mark discovers Petey dead in his apartment and finds a Lumon security card among his belongings
Ms. Cobel arrives at the scene posing as a detective and questions Mark about his relationship with Petey
The severed employees participate in a Music Dance Experience reward session after meeting productivity targets
Dylan enthusiastically engages with the retro dance party while Irving and Helly observe the bizarre corporate entertainment
Mark begins experiencing memory flashes and headaches suggesting his severance barrier may be weakening
Ms. Cobel reviews surveillance footage of the team and discusses Mark's condition with Mr. Milchick
Helly discovers a hidden message from previous employees warning about Lumon's true intentions
The team learns about the four departments: Macrodata Refinement, Optics and Design, General Services, and Biotechnology
Mark's outie visits a doctor about his symptoms while Ms. Cobel secretly monitors his activities outside work
Irving shares his passion for Lumon's corporate history and his collection of company memorabilia with the team
Helly attempts to communicate with her outie by hiding messages in her clothing
Ms. Cobel uses her cover identity to integrate herself into Mark's outside life and gather intelligence
The refinement work reveals strange patterns that the employees cannot understand or remember outside their severed state
Mark experiences a severe episode that causes him to briefly access suppressed memories from his work persona

interactions.description: The viewer knows Cobel is posing as a detective and has infiltrated Mark's outside life; Mark has no idea his neighbor and his interrogator are the same person working for Lumon

All three plotlines turn on the same premise — information is power, and Lumon's control depends on keeping its people ignorant. Helly finds a hidden message, Mark flashes suppressed memory, Cobel surveils to prevent both
Cobel physically enters Mark's outside world, collapsing the boundary between his severed and unsevered life that the entire show's premise depends on keeping separate

The Music Dance Experience puts both Dylan and Irving in relief against each other — Dylan's uncritical absorption into Lumon's rewards versus Irving's detachment hints at two different trajectories away from corporate faith

episodes.theme: The machinery of consent — every system Lumon uses to keep the innies compliant (rewards, protocol, restricted knowledge) is shown fraying at exactly the moment a genuine human need (connection, truth, escape) pushes against it

events.event: The Macrodata Refinement team receives an overtime assignment requiring them to work late into the evening
Irving begins experiencing unusual visions of black paint and floods during work sessions
Ms. Cobel introduces new security measures and monitoring protocols following recent incidents on the severed floor
Helly discovers a map of the office layout and attempts to find alternative escape routes
Irving delivers a handbook to Optics & Design, where the team encounters Burt, and Irving and Burt form an immediate connection
Dylan becomes increasingly competitive about productivity scores and Lumon rewards programs
The team learns about the Perpetuity Wing, a restricted area containing exhibits about Lumon's history and founder Kier Eagan
Ms. Cobel reviews footage of Irving's interaction with Burt and expresses concern about inter-departmental relationships
Helly finds a bathroom stall covered in carved messages from previous employees documenting their suffering
Mark experiences a memory bleed episode during which he recalls fragments of his outside life
Irving begins making excuses to visit Optics & Design and spend time with Burt, violating company protocols
The team discovers that their refinement work involves categorizing numbers based on emotional responses they cannot explain
Ms. Cobel confronts Mark about his deteriorating condition and threatens consequences for the entire team
Dylan receives a special reward but begins questioning whether the promised benefits are worth his sacrifice

interactions.description: Irving believes his visits to Burt are private and personal; Cobel is already reviewing the footage and treating the connection as a security threat Irving cannot see coming
All three innies are reaching toward something real — escape, love, genuine reward — and all three are blocked or poisoned by the same institutional cage, rhyming on the theme of manufactured consent

The bathroom stall messages and the revelation about emotional refinement work converge: both events strip away Lumon's benign facade and confirm that the floor is a place of suffering with hidden purpose
Mark's memory bleed and his team's growing curiosity push toward truth that Cobel is actively suppressing; Mark doesn't know how closely he is being watched
Lumon's reward system, which Cobel enforces as a control mechanism, is the same thing that is beginning to fail on Dylan — the episode quietly tests whether compliance can be bought indefinitely

episodes.theme: The self asserts itself through the body when every other exit is sealed

events.event: Irving makes repeated visits to Optics & Design under work-related pretexts to spend time with Burt
Ms. Cobel increases surveillance on Irving in response to his inter-departmental visits
Ms. Cobel implements new protocols to prevent inter-departmental fraternization
Helly attempts self-harm to force her outie to quit the severance program
Mark intervenes to stop Helly's suicide attempt
The severed floor goes into lockdown following Helly's suicide attempt
Mr. Milchick conducts enhanced wellness sessions to address the team's growing dysfunction and rebellious behavior
Dylan discovers additional reward options and becomes obsessed with earning points for a coveted caricature portrait
The team learns more about Lumon's founder worship and the quasi-religious aspects of corporate culture
Irving experiences intense visions of black paint flooding the office and collapses during work
Ms. Cobel and Mr. Milchick debate how to handle the increasing instability among the severed employees
Burt shows Irving a painting in Optics & Design depicting Lumon's four departments in harmony
Mark begins remembering more details about Petey and their friendship outside the severed environment
Helly's outie sends a video message reaffirming her commitment to the severance program despite her innie's protests
The team processes larger datasets with increasingly emotional responses as refinement work grows more complex
Irving and Burt share a moment of connection that violates multiple company policies regarding workplace relationships

interactions.description: All three innies reach toward something genuinely human — freedom, love, recognition — and all three are rebuffed or distorted by Lumon's architecture; desire itself is the thing the company cannot permit
The viewer sees that Helly's outie has already weaponized Helly's own consent against her; Helly the innie doesn't understand the trap is airtight
Helly's suicide attempt forces Cobel's surveillance apparatus to visibly activate — the two plotlines physically collide in the lockdown and wellness sessions
Irving believes his visits to O&D are private acts of longing; the viewer sees Cobel has already flagged them and is tightening policy around him
Both plotlines hinge on the body as the only instrument of innie agency — Helly uses hers to demand exit, Irving uses his to pursue connection; the body is the one thing Lumon cannot fully sever

episodes.theme: Every form of connection or reward inside Lumon is ultimately owned by the institution and can be revoked the moment it threatens control

events.event: Ms. Cobel discovers Irving and Burt's growing relationship and implements disciplinary measures to separate them
The MDR team learns that Burt is retiring from Lumon
Irving plans a farewell party for Burt in Optics & Design
Dylan earns enough points to claim his coveted reward but finds the experience disappointingly brief and hollow
Mark begins piecing together clues about Petey's reintegration process and the Underground Railroad helping severed employees
Helly receives another message from her outie expressing commitment to the program and dismissing her suffering
Irving organizes an unauthorized gathering in Optics & Design, inviting the entire MDR team to celebrate Burt's retirement
Ms. Cobel monitors the party through surveillance while planning her response to the protocol violation
The team discovers that Optics & Design creates propaganda artwork glorifying Lumon and Kier Eagan's philosophy
Irving and Burt share an intimate moment while examining artwork depicting the company's four departments
Dylan realizes his reward experience was designed to be addictive and meaningless, causing him to question Lumon's motivation system
Mark finds additional evidence of Petey's reintegration attempts and the underground network supporting escaped severed employees
Ms. Cobel interrupts the retirement party and enforces immediate consequences for the unauthorized gathering
Burt departs Lumon, devastating Irving, who experiences another intense vision of the office flooding with black paint
The team returns to regular work duties under increased scrutiny and enhanced security measures

interactions.description: The viewer sees Cobel monitoring the unauthorized party and preparing consequences before Irving and the team know she is watching — their celebration is already doomed while it is happening

All three plotlines stage the same discovery from different angles: Lumon's rewards and relationships are manufactured to be hollow — Burt's departure takes connection away from Irving just as Dylan learns his earned reward was engineered to be empty, and Helly learns her outie has no regard for her suffering

The unauthorized retirement party physically brings the MDR team into Optics and Design, where the truth about Lumon's propaganda is visible, and where Cobel's surveillance and enforcement intersect — Irving's personal farewell becomes the site of both ideological revelation and institutional crackdown
Cobel enforcing order on the floor mirrors Helly's outie enforcing the severance contract — both are authority figures using institutional power to deny an innie any exit or autonomy

episodes.theme: Compliance is a performance — every system of control produces the resistance it was designed to prevent

events.event: Mark discovers a wellness session room where Milchick has been conducting psychological manipulation techniques on Dylan
The team learns about the break room, a white isolation chamber used for psychological reconditioning through repetitive exercises
Helly is sent to the break room as punishment for her continued resistance and must repeatedly apologize for her behavior
Ms. Cobel reveals more aggressive surveillance tactics and begins implementing enhanced control measures across the severed floor
Irving processes Burt's departure while experiencing increasingly vivid hallucinations about black paint and office floods
Mark finds evidence that Petey successfully reintegrated his memories before his death, suggesting severance can be reversed
Dylan begins questioning his loyalty to Lumon after witnessing Helly's treatment in the break room
The team discovers that their refinement work involves processing data related to human emotions and psychological states
Ms. Cobel conducts individual interviews with team members to assess their loyalty and mental stability
Mark experiences breakthrough memories connecting his work and outside identities, causing severe disorientation
Helly emerges from the break room appearing temporarily compliant but secretly more determined to escape
Irving researches Lumon's history and discovers disturbing information about the company's true operations and Kier Eagan's philosophy
The team begins planning coordinated resistance against Lumon's control systems while maintaining the appearance of compliance
Ms. Cobel escalates her monitoring of Mark's outside life and prepares more extreme measures to maintain severance integrity

interactions.description: All three innies are being broken by Lumon in different ways — isolation, witness to cruelty, loss of connection — and each independently arrives at the same threshold: genuine resistance over performance of compliance

Helly's apparent compliance after the break room, Dylan's disillusionment, and Mark's discovery that severance is reversible all converge into the team's coordinated resistance plan — the first episode where all four MDR arcs physically meet in a shared goal

The viewer sees the team beginning to plan coordinated resistance; Cobel believes her enhanced surveillance and loyalty interviews have re-stabilized the floor — she does not yet know a conspiracy is forming beneath her
Mark's breakthrough memory bleed (S01E07#10) is visible to the viewer as evidence severance is cracking; Cobel reads it only as instability to be managed, not as a sign the wall is already failing

Both Irving and Helly lose something real this episode — Burt's presence, bodily autonomy in the break room — and both respond by turning inward toward an obsessive interior state, painting hallucinations and secret resolve respectively

episodes.theme: The self cannot be cleanly divided — what severance steals from one half is felt as a wound in both

events.event: Irving discovers a secret room containing files and equipment suggesting Lumon's true purpose involves consciousness manipulation and control experiments
Mark finds a way to trigger the overtime contingency protocol that temporarily awakens work personalities in the outside world
The team executes their plan during an overtime session, allowing their work selves to experience the outside world for the first time
Mark's work personality awakens in his outside apartment and discovers photographs revealing his deceased wife Gemma worked at Lumon
Dylan's work self finds himself with his family and learns he has a young son he never knew existed
Irving's work personality awakens in his outside home and discovers his outside self has been investigating Lumon and collecting evidence about severed employees
Helly's work self discovers she is Helena Eagan, daughter of Lumon's current CEO and a willing participant in the severance program
Ms. Cobel desperately attempts to contain the situation while the work personalities gather information about their outside lives
Mark discovers that Ms. Selvig, his outie's neighbor, is actually Ms. Cobel monitoring his external activities
Irving finds evidence that his outside self knows other severed employees and has been working to expose Lumon's operations
Dylan's work self experiences his family life and realizes the magnitude of what severance has taken from him
Helena Eagan's work personality confronts the reality of her privileged position and complicity in Lumon's system
The overtime protocol begins failing as the work personalities struggle to return information to their severed selves before the session ends

interactions.description: The viewer learns Helly is Helena Eagan and a willing Lumon participant; Cobel knows but the innie Helly does not — the very identity she wants to escape is one she chose from the outside

Each work self simultaneously discovers a different kind of loss severance has imposed — a dead wife, an unknown son, a secret investigation, a complicit identity — rhyming around the theme that the self divided is the self destroyed
Mark's innie discovers Cobel has been living next door as Ms. Selvig, collapsing the boundary between inside surveillance and outside life into a single moment of recognition

The team executes the protocol while Cobel scrambles to contain it — the viewer sees both sides of the containment race simultaneously, generating dramatic irony about who will prevail before the session expires
Both Irving and Mark discover their outside selves have been independently investigating Lumon — suggesting the hunger to know bleeds across the severance barrier even without conscious memory
